programme.desc: The defendant's lover testify to the jury; Dr. Miller confirms what other witnesses testified to.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Dr. Brad Miller and the Three-Year Affair
programme.desc: Jurors learn about the secret life Melanie McGuire was having outside her marriage with Bill.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: The Motive to Kill the Husband
programme.desc: One of the State's most important witnesses continues his testimony to the jury.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: Almost a year after Bill McGuire was killed police wiretapped the phone calls of her wife, Melanie.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: "Blame the victim" was the theme of Jade Janks's defense as she testified on the stand to jurors.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors were told of the very moment Jade Janks came across naked pictures of herself on her stepdad Tom Merriman's computer. According to the defendant, she feared Merriman's reaction after she came across nude photos of herself on his computer. So, instead of confronting him, Jade Janks sought the services of a security guard, the same guy prosecutors claimed she tried to recruit in her murder plot.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: In The Eight Days To Murder
programme.desc: Jade Janks was emotional as she told the jury that she saw more than 100 nude photos of herself on her stepdad's computer. She claimed that Tom Merriman even cropped some of the pictures of her and divided them up into different folders based on her different body parts. Eight days after she discovered the nude photos on her stepdad's computer Merriman died. During her long testimony to jurors, Janks' defense team wanted her to narrate the days and hours leading up to Tom Merriman's death.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jade Janks had more to say on the stand. During a long direct examination, she told jurors her side of the story of events that led to Tom Merriman's overdose. Was he asleep or was he dead? That's what the defendant recalled when she came across the body of Tom Merriman lying there in her car. Janks tried to convince the jury on her side story on what led to her stepdad's death.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Defense got to cross-examine the police officer who discovered Alex Woodworth's body. The focus was on the details about the bloody crime scene. Alex was only 24 years old when he was stabbed 16 times.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: The jury learned more about the details of Ezra McCandless's hospital visit following the fatal stabbing of Alex Woodworth. Hours after the killing, a nurse gave Ezra a sexual assault exam. She told the nurse that she didn't remember being raped.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: The sexual assault exam on Ezra McCandless took four hours. It included flossing for DNA collection, a vaginal swab and even fingernail evidence. And all of this went into the police report for this murder case.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Defense had some questions for the nurse who performed the sexual assault exam on Ezra McCandless. She had said she thought she had been raped, despite no memory of the fatal encounter with Alex Woodworth.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.desc: In 2019, a notorious crime ripped a Texas community apart as a bad break-up left a beloved teacher stabbed to death and a former high school football player convicted of the brutal slaying.

programme.desc: David Scott challenges Halford on her role that fateful night and holds her accountable.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.sub-title: Deal With The Devil - Maggie Ybarra
programme.desc: Nobody knew about suburban dad Michael Hendricks' dark sexual fantasies or his dangerous dalliance with Maggie Ybarra. Behind closed doors, Hendricks and Ybarra nurtured a secret relationship that would turn deadly. In her first-ever interview, Ybarra reveals to host David Scott what it was like to fall under the spell of the Devil Next Door - and how their twisted partnership ended with abusive mind games, a shocking murder, and an innocent life destroyed.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: While jogging along a scenic canal, a young woman is brutally attacked and raped by a stranger. Police use groundbreaking genetic genealogy to identify the perpetrator.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 2003, a man walking in the Sierra Nevada mountains finds a human skull. In nearby Mammoth Lakes, a family is reeling after a young mother mysteriously skips town. It'll take revolutionary science to piece this puzzle together - 20 years later.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: When a beloved young woman is brutally murdered and left in an abandoned house in Brown County, Texas, investigative genetic genealogy could be the key to finding the perpetrator.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: This episode follows the exhaustive investigation into the murder of Tangie Simms and how genetic genealogy helped solve the crime.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In Willoughby, Ohio, Mark Madger comes home to find his wife Nadine lying in a pool of her own blood, stabbed to death. Four decades later, cutting-edge DNA science provides answers the Madger family have been waiting a lifetime for.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1976, 16-year-old Pamela Maurer leaves a friend's house to walk to a nearby location for a soft drink. She never makes it home. The next morning, road workers discover her body along College Road. New DNA technology solves the case in 2019.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1990 Mississippi, a woman is kidnapped and raped at knifepoint. Police are unable to identify the perpetrator. It'll take forward-thinking investigators, state-of-the-art science and brave survivors to put this man behind bars.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: This episode follows the exhaustive investigation into the murder of Tangie Simms and how genetic genealogy helped solve the crime.

programme.desc: Police work for decades with emerging forensic sciences to identify the killer of two women in Lake Tahoe.

programme.desc: A woman's skeletal remains are found in Okaloosa County in 1994, sparking a years-long search for her identity and killer - until a new forensic tool provides answers.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1979, 16-year-old Kim Bryant disappears from outside a Dairy Queen in Las Vegas. Three weeks later, her body is found: she had been sexually assaulted and bludgeoned to death. 40 years later, genetic genealogy finally names the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1987, the body of Darlene Krashoc is discovered behind a Colorado Springs restaurant. She had been savagely beaten, raped and strangled. Investigators struggle to identify this barbaric killer. 30 years later, science provides a breakthrough.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.sub-title: Left for Dead in Logan County

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The NorCal Rapist terrorizes the women of North California for years until forensic science can identify him.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.sub-title: The Hair That Called His Bluff
programme.desc: A single strand of hair, a DNA match and decades of detective work finally solve the murder of schoolgirl Rashell Ward after over 40 years.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A town in Washington lives in fear after a 17-year-old girl is abducted and raped without a suspect in sight.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1999, hospital worker Jennifer Watkins is sexually assaulted and murdered. 20 years later, revolutionary DNA testing helps to solve the cold case.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, three people were shot to death at a Georgetown coffee shop. Investigators could find no immediate suspect or no clear motive for the murders. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong or an act of cold-blooded vengeance?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A loner and distrustful of authority, Claude Dallas lived off the land, poaching wildlife and selling the hides. When two game wardens confronted Dallas about his deeds, they were shot and killed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a parole officer was found shot dead in his car, the FBI, DEA and NYPD struggled to contain the mounting violence from drug gangs that continued to escalate. Authorities sought to put two ruthless drug kingpins behind bars for the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1985, a Lebanese terrorist and his four accomplices hijacked Royal Jordanian Flight 405 before taking off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Amid the cocaine wars of 1980s Miami, a multi-agency tactical squad suspected that a group of cops might be involved in illegal drug busts. The squad uncovered a ring of police who were making millions by ripping off smuggled cocaine.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A special task force was formed in the early 1990s to combat the flow of Colombia cocaine into New York. As part of an elaborate sting operation, one detective went undercover and infiltrated a drug gang connected to Pablo Escobar's Medellin cartel.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: For Tammy Brannen, Christmas was always a special time until her daughter was gone without warning in 1989.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A class field trip ended with an 11-year-old girl missing. When the parents failed to locate their daughter in their quiet Illinois town, the FBI joined the investigation, working with friends and family in an urgent search to find the missing girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1998, a wealthy woman disappeared from her home in Jackson, Mississippi. The only clue was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names but no specific instructions. The FBI struggled to find answers in the cryptic message.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, Florida's cocaine imports were among the highest in the nation and regional territories were enforced with intimidation and murder. One dealer would stop at nothing to keep FBI agents from building a case against him.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In New York, an armored car robbery left one guard dead and another critically wounded. The FBI suspected domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. Their investigation led them to the Black Liberation Army and the Weather Underground.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990s Philadelphia, a ruthless grab for power led to a full-scale mob war. Using informants and high-tech surveillance, the FBI infiltrated the most powerful mob family in the city and sought to bring down the organization.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, Jamie McMahan and Christopher Kauffman went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A stranger snatched an 8-year-old girl as she played in her Tucson, Arizona neighborhood. Within hours, police began to suspect a drifter who was out on parole for abducting and molesting children.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1986, a psychotic killer shot and killed a police officer at point-blank range, leading investigators in a wild chase across the Midwest. As his tally of murders and abductions grew, the FBI raced against time to put a stop to the deranged gunman.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, two men used a sledgehammer to break into a Camden, New Jersey home. Police tracked the suspects to a nearby park but the criminals were able to escape after a nearly fatal shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1980, a maid discovered that a copy machine, placed in a busy Las Vegas casino, was actually a bomb.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of the serial killer who worked as a nurse in Texas, where she killed many children and infants by injecting them with drugs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look into the case of a sadistic killer who posed as an MI5 agent in order to torture and murder his victim.

programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the woman who was driven into a jealous rage and stabbed her sister to death while her children slept upstairs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2022, Danville Neil was finally jailed for the brutal 1993 murders of a WWII veteran and his sister.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jerome Brudos was a serial killer and necrophile who murdered four women in Oregon during the 1960s. He was known as the 'The Lust Killer' and 'The Shoe Fetish Slayer.'

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Ali Qazimaj is an Albanian conman who brutally murdered an elderly couple in 2016.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A convicted killer serving life for manslaughter is found to be guilty of a second murder 32 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Police in Aurora, Colorado found the body of a young man in in his own home with a bullet wound to the head.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Rocky Beamon lures a woman into the woods and drowns her. Once in prison, he brutally kills two fellow inmates - and vows to kill again.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: After shooting two people, Rodney Berget kidnaps and sexually assaults a gas station clerk. Prison struggles to contain him: an escape expert, his final attempt involves the sadistic murder of a corrections officer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Denver Simmons is as dangerous as he is ruthless. Incarcerated for murdering a woman and her 14-year-old son, his violence only spiraled further once he was behind bars.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Convicted of shooting a man and 21 other felonies, Miti Maugaotega Jr. receives one of the longest prison sentences in Hawaiʻi history. Behind bars, he joins a prison gang and becomes a tornado of death and destruction.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Gloria, a devoted mother, tried to stop her son David's forbidden relationship. Her attempt to protect her family ended in tragedy as David's hidden violent streak erupted with devastating consequences.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jordan Monaghan killed his 24-day-old daughter Ruby on New Year's Day in January 2013. In August of that year, when his partner tried to end their relationship, he killed his son Logan, who was 21 months old.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a tragic twist of fate, just days after the woman sold her home and moved to a modest trailer, a fire took both the trailer and her life. But the autopsy proved this was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run boating accident caused a death, police must search for one boat among 1200 others.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When court clerk Peter Porco doesn't report to his work in November 2004, a courts officer is ordered to the Porcos' family home in Bethlehem, NY.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A security guard disappeared from his post without a trace; his remains were found a year later in a remote camp site.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a missing college girl and discover her double life: attending school by day and working as a call-girl by night. Her body is found and investigators find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car, the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One warm summer afternoon, the town of Verona, Wisconsin faced its first triple homicide. To solve the case, investigators had to delve into the world of high rollers and offshore betting.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 1995 fire razes the mansion of a Kansas doctor, killing two of her three children; disturbing accounts from an ex-husband lead investigators to believe the doctor set the blaze herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On Halloween night 2004, Adriane Insogna and Leslie Mazzara were brutally murdered in their Napa home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Survivors of Nazi atrocities recognized Archbishop Trifa as having led an attack against Jews during WWII.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, Patrick McRae was found dead in his Des Moines home and the crime scene was awash with blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people in Seattle, Washington died after taking an over-the-counter pain reliever. Lab analysis of the pills showed they were tainted with a lethal concentration of cyanide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: With no forensic evidence inside a murder scene, investigators were baffled. But they suspected that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A body is found stuffed into a suitcase forcing the police to search though the landfill for another missing victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a wealthy American businessman was found in his rental car. Bone fragments were the only remains.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2007, Shamaia Smith disappeared from the strip club she worked at in East Hartford, CT. Police look at various customers of the strip club, but focus on local businessman Kenneth Otto.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police get a clue about a couple who disappeared on Memorial Day weekend during an in-progress robbery.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: No one in a residential community saw anything the day one of their neighbors was brutally murdered. Fingerprints found at the crime scene helped investigators to apprehend the presumed killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A car carrying three men pulls up alongside another on an Alaskan highway and fires shots, leaving a passenger dead. One of the passengers in the killer's car agrees to testify against his friends. The resulting trials don't end the carnage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A go-go dancer told her family she needed to get away for a while and then she simply disappeared. Investigators were suspicious, because she took neither her car nor her clothes. 20 years later, her family received a call that changed everything.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Security cameras in a casino tracked a young woman's movements until shortly before she disappeared. She was never seen again.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On the way to Yosemite National Park, a car carrying three Secret Service agents collided with another car.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How did the stalker obtain the security system code for his victim's home? How did he steal her personal photographs? Police needed answers and they found them in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on the bedroom floor of her apartment. The crime scene yielded little of value and investigators wondered if they would find enough evidence to make a case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, college co-ed Jenna Verhaalen was found dead in her Bryan, Texas apartment. Petechial hemorrhages in her eyes indicated that she was strangled.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Michigan State University grad student disappeared and was presumed dead. With the help of a professor of geological sciences, police hoped to get the "dirt" on her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men called police to report the same murder. The investigation uncovered more unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Genell Plude is found dead in her bathroom, the scene points to suicide. But a coroner's inquest and a unique application of forensic science gave investigators a different explanation for her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The only clue recovered from a shooting is a twelve-guage shotgun. Scientists use unique methods to lift the serial number from the weapon to trace it to its owner

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While Earl Morris was vacationing in California, he learned his wife Ruby Morris had gone missing from their home in Arizona. The results of the investigation surprised everyone...especially Earl Morris.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One investigator's quick thinking helps him capture a shoe impression made in snow before it melts away.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: They thought the fire would cover their crime. But one tiny clue led to a trio of killers behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, Mary Ann Clibbery was found brutally murdered in her Illinois business and investigators had to determine if this was a robbery gone wrong or a calculated murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Detectives search for the bombers of two churches in Illinois, hoping that the materials used in the remnants of the handmade bombs will offer the clues to catch the culprits.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A cop is found dead. His wife first says the motive was robbery; then she tells police it was suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A beverage company pleads guilty to violating federal safety laws when a baby dies from E. coli after drinking apple juice.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman disappeared without a trace, there were two possible explanations: kidnapping or murder.
